title: Repair archive index ownership evidence

sources.reason: Repository rules requiring generated status, Work Item evidence, and append-only archive handling.
Generated archive discovery index whose ownership must be covered by the Work Item that causes its append-only update.
Prior correction evidence showing the archive record that exposed the index ownership gap.

problemStatement: The PR aggregate correctly attributes the documentation change but reports the append-only archive index as unowned. The archive index must be associated with a Work Item scope through generated evidence rather than hand-edited.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The archive index update is owned by this Work Item after finish and the PR aggregate has no unowned paths.
No prior archived Work Item record is edited.
evidence: git diff comparison against the prior correction commit

scenarioCoverage.scenario: No documentation, runtime, schema, release, or test behavior changes.
scenarioCoverage.reason: This Work Item changes only generated governance ownership evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: Governance-record-only correction; risk is limited to archive ownership and append-only index consistency.

executionDecision.reason: The ownership gap, generated-index boundary, and verification evidence are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Review that only the generated archive index and this Work Item's records are attributed, with no prior archive edits.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The generated archive index update is covered by this Work Item's scope and the final PR aggregate reports no unowned paths.
The prior archived Contract/Summary records remain unchanged and no archive file under .ai/work-items/archive/2026/** is edited.
No documentation, runtime, schema, release workflow, or test file is changed.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: PR ownership validation treats the append-only archive index as an unowned change when a Work Item's scope covers only its active records. The repository needs a generated ownership record whose scope explicitly includes the index.
constraints: Do not hand-edit the archive index or any prior archived Work Item record.
Keep the correction limited to generated governance evidence; do not touch product documentation, source, tests, schemas, CI, or Make targets.
intent.rationale: Making the generated index part of the Work Item scope lets archive ownership be derived from the same evidence chain as the archived Contract/Summary while preserving append-only history.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
